entry.title: Re: Issues with Archiving Batches
entry.content: I tried to archive these notifications with SARA and object QM_QMEL, then I looked for the material in QMEL table and found two notifications with the problrmatic badge but both of them are closed.

entry.title: Re: Name of iOS app is not showing correctly on iOS and MacOS
entry.content:
Hi,

We were able to pinpoint the problem to the single quote in the name. We'll look to fix the issue. In the meantime, as a temporary workaround, you can drop or replace the single quote in d'Italia to avoid the fallback naming.

BR,
Pekka

entry.title: Re: Repricing at VF04
entry.content: Hi, the (re) pricing should be done during invoice creation based on the pricing type of the copy control

entry.title: Re: How to use the Libraries effectively in SAP Cloud ALM?

For now, we encourage you to keep exploring and sharing your feedback—it helps shape the evolution of Cloud ALM to better meet user needs.

As long as it aligns with my responsibilities at work, you can count on it 😊

Thanks so much for your detailed reply. Cheers and see you in the council call.

Bruno

entry.title: Re: Active Ingredient Management in S/4HANA Cloud Public...
entry.content: Many thanks for the quick and correct response.

entry.title: Re: How to use the Libraries effectively in SAP Cloud ALM?
entry.content: PS: Oops, I don't have access to the council call.

entry.title: Re: 999 – The Number of the BSEG
entry.content:
Hello Aldo, 

Thanks for the comment, it’s a valid question which I’d like to clarify again; please let me answer in two parts: 

  1. In 3.1i (and the whole of R/3 and ECC), BSEG compression inevitably meant throwing away possibly valuable information! You had to clear profit center, cost center, profitability segment (PAOBJNR), etc. from BSEG to achieve an effective compression. And BSEG was basically all you had in FI, it was the FI document, the base for your accounting line item reporting, the “FI truth”. With NewGL – introduced around 2004 – this aspect got even worse: you weren’t allowed any longer to clear split criteria like profit center from BSEG (because BSEG was also the base for reconstructing the NewGL records FAGLFLEXA/FAGLFLEXT).  So BSEG compression in R/3 and ECC was always a trade-off, a compromise between getting under the 999 threshold and not losing information: a “workaround”. With S/4HANA and the ACDOCA, this has completely changed, which I tried to explain in this blog post. 
  2. So yes: the technique of BSEG summarization (compression) is almost 30 years old (customizing OBCY/OBCYX, OSS note 36353). But if you read carefully through this note (or other similar notes), you’ll find lots of warning signs, stop signs, pitfalls like “if you use funds management and compress BSEG, the sky’s gonna fall on you”, “if you do this and don’t do that for a customer invoice in an Indian company code, we’re all gonna die”...our experience from scanning hundreds of customer incidents was just that customers were very reluctant to use the classical BSEG summarization, particularly because of the many pitfalls, and understandably so. That’s why we came up with the BAdi mentioned above. It is very convenient, just one FALSE/TRUE-flag needs to be returned, and most importantly: all these restrictions, preconditions, pitfalls will be checked automatically by the system, as a customer you don’t have to worry, you can’t do anything wrong! 

Kind regards,  

Stefan

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning Hub 2024 Update FAQs
entry.content:
Hallo, kurze Fragen hierzu:

Was ist der Unterschied zwischen SAP Learning Hub bisher – also was genau ist neu?

Hier auf Folie 14  stellen wir den Umfang im Vergleich ​
SAP Learning Hub bisher vs. ​SAP Learning Hub neu​ dar.

Der Link zur Folie verweist offenbar auf eine SAP interne Seite, die ich als externer (mit LH Lizenz) nicht anschauen kann - Könntet Ihr ggf. die Folie(n) auch über einen für extern zugänglichen Link bereitstellen?

Weiterhin: Könntet Ihr in die FAQ eine Hinweis aufnehmen, wie die 4 inkludierten Zerti-Teilnahmen gebucht werden können?

Und schliesslich: könnt Ihr auch einen Hinwesi in die FAQ aufnehmen, welche Trainings oder Test-system Zugänge ggf. in der LH Lizenz inkludiert sind? 

Danke

VG J

entry.title: Re: Best Approach for Changing Connection from Quality to...
entry.content:
Hello William,

Thank you for your reply.

However, it seems that it dosen't work for us 😞

I forgot to mention that the data models are not live but reply on an import connection that includes various transformations. There are more than 30 data modes, so it would be an overhead to recreate them all from scratch. Also, copying the model wouldn't help because queries are not transferred in the copy.

Is there any other idea?

entry.title: Re: How can you make a button accessible only for a specific login ID in a custom Z report in SAP AB
entry.content:
Hello,

in addition to what @Sandra_Rossi already has written, I would like to add, that hiding / enabling functions depending on a user ID I consider as a really bad idea and a bad design. If there are reasons for offering functions to specific people (maybe admins), then this should be implemented by authorizations and not depending on user ID's.

Kind regards

Jan

entry.title: Re: Savely secure Credentials in ABAP
entry.content: Do not use SECSTORE: "For legal reasons, only SAP applications may use the secure storage."

A very good understanding blog!

Just one question, where can we get the questionnaire? Is there an official page to download the questionnaire?

Thanks for your help.

Shawn

entry.title: md04 MF331
Re: ABAPConf 2025
entry.content: Tolle Veranstaltung, ich freue mich schon darauf!

entry.title: Re: Seamless planning between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere is coming! Controlled release i
entry.content:
Hi @LeonelAndrade 

Please note that you can complete all necessary steps from the SAP Analytics Cloud admin UI. You need to be system owner of both SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. Also note that seamless planning is not configured via the Product Switch setting but via the Data Storage for Planning setting.

If you configured the Data Storage for Planning setting and it was not successful or you cannot select SAP Datasphere as storage location when creating a model, please open a ticket. 

 

BR
Max

entry.title: Re: Calculating Energy Consumption in the Product Carbon Footprint
entry.content:
I'm not sure if I got the question here. The allocation of the energy used within the production line down to products is done via the resource usage. In the breakdown you then see the different emission sources. 

For more detailed information on the product emissions and the different influencing factors may this roadmap item will help -

  • Find information on various item types, such as energy sources, business activities, purchased products, and transport
  • View specific data points for each line item
  • Increase transparency in the detailed footprint items that contribute to a product’s overall footprint

entry.title: how do we automate space & org members access in SAP BTP cockpit(Cloud foundry env)
entry.content: needs options to automate BTP space & org member access either using some tools or in a different way to reduce manual intervention every time.

entry.title: Re: Information Needed: Cancel Open Quantity Button Behav...
entry.content: Hi, I see you mentioned 'the 'Cancel open Quantity' button in Purchase Request remains enabled even after PostGoodsIssue.' I confirmed the same in the system and informed our development team check it. Ideally with 2411's change, the option should be disabled for the PR has no open quantity. Thanks for the feedback!

entry.title: Re: Are necessary to stop ASE server and replication in o...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

A few comments from me:

sybmgmtdb does not participate in replication, so no worries about the operations on it which won't affect replication and application.

Moreover, you can take a look at this KB on how to handle table growth in sybmgmtdb
https://me.sap.com/notes/2361809

 

Tony

entry.title: How to transfer a custom field from Sales Order to invoice via new project billing
entry.content:
***Image/data in this Blog is from SAP internal systems, sample data, or demo systems. Any resemblance to real data is purely coincidental.***

I am currently working in the Professional Services area of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Recently, we have received multiple customer inquiries regarding the process of transferring custom fields from Sales Orders to invoices using the new project billing functionality. In response to these queries, I have documented my testing within our internal system. It is my hope that this documentation provides valuable insights and guidance for our customers and colleagues.

In this blog, I use the transfer from Sales Order item to Invoice item as a sample. If you wish to transfer a custom field from the Sales Order header to the Invoice header, please enable the scenario 'Sales Order Header to Billing Document Header for Project Billing' during step 1.2. Additionally, pay special attention to step 2 as detailed in my explanation.

Let’s look at the detailed steps on how this can be done.

1. Create the custom field in app "Custom Fields":
1.1 Create the custom field using business context Project Billing Element, click Create and Edit.


1.2 In tab Business Scenarios, enable the scenario 'Sales Order Item to Billing Document Item for Project Billing' if you would like to transfer the customer field to invoice item, or 'Sales Order Header to Billing Document Header for Project Billing' if it's to invoice header.

1.3 Click on Save and you will get below 2 additional custom fields with dependencies, the status is 'Not Published'.

1.4 Go into Business Context 'Sales: Sales Document Item', in tab User Interfaces, enable the service 'Plan Customer Projects' and GUI 'Create, Change, or Display Sales Document(Item)'. Save.


1.5 Go into Business Context 'Sales: Billing Document Item', in tab User Interfaces, enable the service 'Manage Billing Documents' and GUI 'Create, Change, or Display Sales Document(Item)'. Save and Publish.

1.6 You will see the following 3 published custom fields.

2. Add the custom field into the app.
Add the custom field into the item under Billing tab of a customer project as the item corrresponds to the sales order item, enter the value of customer field and Save.
If you wish to add the custom field into header of Billing tab which corresponds to the sales order header, click on Personalized icon and select 'Adapt UI' to include it.
When you adapt the UI to display your custom fields, you have to do this once in the display mode and once in the edit mode.

3. Verify in VA03.
Verfity that the custom field has been populated at the Sales Order item level using VA03.


4. Submit PBR to create BDR.
Go to app "Manage Project Billing", select the Billing Element, click on Prepare Billing and submit the PBR.
Sequence BDR is created.

5. Create invoice in app "Create Billing Document".

6. Verify the custom field in BDR using VFR3.

7. Verify the custom field in invoice using VF03.


Conclusion:
The custom field has been successfully transferred from Sales Order item to the invoice through new project billing process.

Reference:
3015693 - Custom fields are empty in the billing document when using Project Billing functionality
3563126 - The value of custom field is not getting pulled from customer project to related sales order table
Sales Order to Invoice

entry.title: Re: I can't create billing document
entry.content: Please check this note : https://me.sap.com/notes/3331525

entry.title: Configuration for sending Purchase order in XML format to a middleware using OPD
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts

Regarding our requirements, we need to implement an SAP S/4HANA on-premise solution to send a purchase order in XML format via the EDI channel in OPD. The purchase order will be transmitted to a middleware system, rather than directly to the vendor.

We would appreciate your guidance on the following questions:

  1. Can a purchase order be sent to a third-party system in XML format via the EDI channel in OPD? If so, is this still feasible if the third-party system is defined as a logical system rather than a vendor?
  2. Based on our requirements, can this process be fully managed using OPD, or would it be necessary to switch to the NAST method?

Additionally, I would be grateful if you could provide a relevant SAP Note or a detailed tutorial outlining the necessary steps for implementation.

entry.title: Deployment issue on SAP BAS: The value ov "value" is out of range
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I've recently moved moy Frontend development environment form SAP WebIDE to BAS. And unfortunately I'm having a few issues that I don't really understand.

Whenever I try to deploy my application, I recieve a Node.js Error:

info abap-deploy-task /TBP/TEST Creating archive with UI5 build result.
info abap-deploy-task /TBP/TEST Archive created.
error builder:custom deploy-to-abap The value of "value" is out of range. It must be >= 0 and <= 65535. Received 85528
Command deploy failed with error : The value of "value" is out of range. It must be >= 0 and <= 65535. Received 85528
npm verbose cwd /home/user/projects/SCAPP
npm verbose os Linux 6.6.63-cloud-amd64
npm verbose node v20.18.1
npm verbose npm  v10.8.2
npm verbose exit 1
npm verbose code 1
user: SCAPP $

Does anybody know what this means and how I can fix this? Or at least how I can get a better Error Message? I've already uped the log level to 6

Best Wishes

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Management Layer for SAP S/4HANA – FAQ: What is the Difference Between the Enterprise
entry.content:
Hi Shawn,

The enterprise management layer is provided through a service by SAP Services only, hence, if you and your company are interested you shall reach out to your appointed SAP Sales Account Executive (SAE), who will guide you and get in touch with our team.

Regards

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Refreshing the Consumption Item Mapping when a Field...
entry.content:
Hello Ines and Doris,

the goal is to modify a consumption item mapping.   My recommendation is to generate a new chargeable item class from the last version of the consumption class. To do that  (let say you have a consumption item class CoItCl, a chargeable item class ChItCl and a consumption item mapping CoItMa between CoItCl and ChItCl) : 

  1. Create a fake chargeable item class ChItCl_TMP
  2. Replace all the reference to ChItCl  in your charge plans by a reference to ChItCl_TMP
  3. Delete the consumption item mapping CoItMa
  4. Delete the chargeable item class ChItCl  
  5. Create a new  consumption item mapping CoItMa by generating a new chargeable item class ChItCl 
  6. Replace all the reference to ChItCl_TMP in your charge plans by a reference to ChItCl
  7. Delete the chargeable item class ChItCl_TMP

This a little bit time consuming but by doing this you don't have to delete charge plan. 
It has to be clear that this procedure is valid for non-productive environment. For productive environment, in order to be sure to handle all items, you have to think about adding a new consumption item class instead of modifying it. 

Best regards 

Eric F.

entry.title: COND_COUNT - Cannot convert 123 into NUM: loss of significant digits. SAP NCo issue.
entry.content:
Hello,

Recently we started getting "Cannot convert 123 into NUM: loss of significant digits." error from NCo library for the specific field - COND_COUNT from BAPICOND structure. 

By default the length of this field is 2 digits, but the thing is we have some cases when amount of price conditions is more then 99 and that's why it fails now.

As I understood this field controls the order of price conditions and quite important to get a correctly price calculated.

We are using: BAPI_QUOTATION_CREATEFROMDATA2 function to create quote and one of the input parameter there is COND_COUNT.

Can someone please advice what is possible solution here if you faced this issue also?
I'm wondering is it possible to increase COND_COUNT length to 3 or 4 digits and what is the impact of this change on SAP system?

Maybe there is another way how to make it work with the default field length, what is the general/suggested approach in this type of issue?

Sap NCo version is - 3.1.0.42.

 

Thanks in advance,

Best regards,

Dmitriy

entry.title: SAP BTP Exploration Workshop: Nomad HackFest, the first SAP student hackathon in Kazakhstan
entry.content:
The hackathon took place 30 September - 22 November 2024.

The event  included two general parts:

  • September 30 - Opening Ceremony.
  • November 22 - Final projects presentation/defense.

In the hackathon participated students of the 4th year of bachelor's degree and 1-2 years of master's degree. 

For two months, the teams worked on developing innovative solutions based on real business cases provided by SAP clients in the Republic of Kazakhstan, including companies such as Kazakhmys Corporation LLP, Karmet JSC and Bereke Bank JSC.

The participants gained industry knowledge and worked on business tasks: from robotization of business processes to creating mobile applications and using artificial intelligence.

SAP, customers and partners provided the teams with support and resources such as mentoring, on-site training, access to online training and the SAP Build workspace, consultation with industry experts, and opportunities to prototype their ideas on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

Students got a great opportunity to use all the results obtained during this event in their work on diploma projects and master's theses.

The winners were given employment opportunities with partners and clients, as well as a possibilities to complete the implementation of the ideas proposed at the hackathon as part of a project team at an SAP clients.

For more information please refer to the event page : SAP BTP Exploration Workshop: Nomad HackFest | Hackathon Kazakhstan 2024

entry.title: Re: Display PE51 Payslip on Adobe From
entry.content:
So can you confirm that you haven't considered

  • Using the BAPI_GET_PAYSLIP_PDF (cause you want to create a form outside of standard?)
  • Using the FM HRO1_GET_FUNAME to get the name of a FM that will generate the raw data, then calling your own Adobe form on that data (cause you don't want to call unreleased FM?)

entry.title: Re: Bank accounts cannot be deleted
entry.content: Hello Fabio, Thank you very much for this information. But actually I would like to remove the bank accounts that have been created in the vendor or customer master record. For example, the bank account for a customer changes. We then want to delete the old bank account and enter the new one. Now, however, we can only adjust the validity of the old bank account. Is there no way to delete the old ones? It works on the test system, but not on the prod. I get the message: Data cannot be deleted because deletion is deactivated. Best regards, Güli

entry.title: SAP PO 7.5 SP24 Collapse Under 800MB-1GB Messages & 500QPS - Need Interface-Level Throttling Guidanc
entry.content:
Problem Description:
Our SAP PO system (4-node cluster, 128GB RAM per node, NW 7.5 SP24) recently crashed due to 800MB-1GB payloads combined with 500QPS on specific ICO interfaces. The root cause traces to uncontrolled message volume/size overwhelming the system, despite our "aggressive" global configuration:

 

  • max_message_size = 1GB (yes, I know this is risky 😰)
  • Standard message processing without interface-specific throttling

 

Key Challenges:

 

  1. No Interface-Level Controls: SAP Help/notes (e.g., 2503816, 2761288) only describe global message size/QPS limits. We need per-ICO interface rules (e.g., "Interface X: max 500MB payload, 100QPS").
  2. Scalability Gap: 4 nodes with 128GB RAM should handle large messages, but high concurrency + payloads cause JVM OOMs (heap dumps show 90%+ usage during peaks).
  3. Business Impact: These interfaces process critical EDI/XML files from logistics partners – we can’t reduce payload size or QPS without re-architecting upstream systems.

 

Asking the Community:

 

  • Has anyone implemented interface-specific throttling (QPS/message size) in PO 7.5?
  • Are there hidden configuration parameters or custom BAdIs for ICO-level controls?
  • For large message handling: Any best practices beyond  (e.g., disk-based processing, async queues, chunking)?
  • Could SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) sidecar help offload large messages while keeping PO for legacy logic?

 

System Details:

 

  • NW PO 7.5 SP24 (nwa 7.5 SP24)
  • 4x nodes (VMs: 32vCPU, 128GB RAM, SSD)
  • All ICO interfaces use synchronous processing (due to partner requirements)
  • Message types: XML/EDI (uncompressed, no attachments)

 

Appreciate any insights – we’re currently firefighting with temporary circuit breakers and need a sustainable solution!

entry.title: Re: How to create a value help for custom and released domain fixed values?
entry.content:
Works fine if you only need to display the Key value on UI,

But you cannot  display description instead of key, by using associations as Projection View does not allow associations from custom entity. 

Error Message

Target of association _associationName cannot be an abstract/custom entity (ZHP_I_DOMAIN_FIX_VAL)

my SAP Datasphere trial account doesnt work, because after the login I always get logged out from the system. Can anybody help me here?

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Default value for buyer (old OMFI) in S/4HANA Cloud P...
entry.content:
Hi,

Unfortunately, no, there is no solution yet in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

entry.title: Integrating SAP Sales Cloud V2 and SAP Emarsys via SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog post provides a step-by-step guide on integrating SAP Sales Cloud V2 with SAP Emarsys using the SAP Integration Suite. The focus is on setting up a straightforward integration flow (iFlow) to extract contact data from SAP Sales Cloud V2 and create corresponding contacts in SAP Emarsys.

The goal is to demonstrate how to connect both systems, using APIs and middleware to automate data transfer. This serves as a foundation for more complex integrations in the future, making it easier to extend the process with additional functionality. A key aspect of this integration is OpenID Connect (OIDC, OAuth 2.0, JWT) based API authentication, which is required to securely access SAP Emarsys. We will cover how authentication works in this context and how to configure it properly within the iFlow.

 

Prerequisites

Before implementing this iFlow ensure that you have the following:

  • An active SAP Integration Suite tenant with Cloud Integration enabled
  • Access to a SAP Sales Cloud V2 account with API permissions
  • Access to an SAP Emarsys account with API permissions

 

Implementation

Overview of the Integration Flow

Overview of the iFlow between SAP Sales Cloud V2 and SAP Emarsys using SAP Integration SuiteThe diagram above provides an overview of the iFlow, which connects SAP Sales Cloud V2 with SAP Emarsys using the Cloud Integration component within the SAP Integration Suite. This process automates retrieving contact data from SAP Sales Cloud V2, processes the information, and transfers it to SAP Emarsys to create corresponding contacts.

  1. Start Timer – Initiates the iFlow execution at predefined intervals.
  2. Set Filter – Applies conditions to select only relevant contacts from SAP Sales Cloud V2.
  3. Get Sales Cloud Contacts – Fetches contact data via the SAP Sales Cloud API.
  4. Extract Sales Cloud Contacts – Processes and structures the extracted data.
  5. Configure SAP Emarsys Request –Prepares the Request and Setting Up OAuth Authentication
  6. Create Contacts in SAP Emarsys – Sends the contact data via an API request to SAP Emarsys.

In the following sections, each step of the iFlow will be explained in detail.

Step 1: Start Timer - Triggering the Integration Flow

Timer Configuration – Scheduling the iFlow to run at regular intervals

The iFlow is initiated by a timer-based trigger, which ensures that contact data is regularly synchronized between SAP Sales Cloud V2 and SAP Emarsys.

As shown in the screenshot, the timer is configured using a Simple Schedule, where the iFlow runs every hour between February 27, 2025, at 09:00 UTC and March 6, 2025, at 18:00 UTC.

Step 2: Set Filter - Filtering Relevant Contacts

Configure SAP Sales Cloud V2 Request

In this step, we apply a filter to ensure that only the necessary contacts are fetched from SAP Sales Cloud V2. First, we limit the selection to contacts associated with the United States. Second, we include only those that have been newly created or updated within the last hour.

After adding the timer to trigger the iFlow, the next step is to insert a Content Modifier to define the query parameters for retrieving the relevant contact data.

  1. Add a Content Modifier
  2. Navigate to the Message Header tab.
  3. Create a new header entry:
    • Action: Create
    • Name: CamelHttpQuery (you can choose your own name)
    • Source Type: Expression
    • Source Value: $filter=isContactPersonFor/country eq 'US' and (adminData/createdOn gt '${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd}T${date:now:HH}:00:00Z' or adminData/updatedOn gt '${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd}T${date:now:HH}:00:00Z'})

The filter dynamically adjusts the timestamp to the beginning of the current hour, ensuring that each execution of the integration flow retrieves only the latest contact records.

Step 3: Get Sales Cloud Contacts - Fetching Data from SAP Sales Cloud V2

To retrieve contact data from SAP Sales Cloud V2, we need to set up a Request Reply and connect it to an HTTP Receiver Adapter. This ensures that the iFlow sends a request to SAP Sales Cloud V2 and processes the response dynamically.

1. Setting Up the Request Reply Step

The Request Reply step is required to handle synchronous communication with SAP Sales Cloud. It ensures that the request is sent, and the response is processed before moving to the next step in the iFlow.

  1. Add a Request Reply step after the Content Modifier.
  2. Connect the Request Reply step to an HTTP Receiver Adapter.

2. Configuring the HTTP Receiver Adapter

The HTTP Receiver Adapter is responsible for sending the request to SAP Sales Cloud V2. The configuration is as follows:

Configure the connection to SAP Sales Cloud V2

  • Address: The endpoint for retrieving contact persons: 
    https://mytenant.crm.cloud.sap/sap/c4c/api/v1/contact-person-service/contactPersons
  • Method: GET is selected to fetch data.
  • Proxy Type: Set to Internet, ensuring external API communication.
  • Authentication: Basic
  • Credentials: Since SAP Sales Cloud V2 requires authentication, we must configure Basic Authentication in the Security Material section of the SAP Integration Suite.
    • Set up Basic Credentials in the SAP Integration Suite: Go to "Monitor" → "Security Material" → Create a new "User Credentials" → enter a Credential Name, Username and Password → add the Credential Name in HTTP Receiver Adapter

The dynamic filter query that was set up in the Content Modifier is passed automatically in the request to ensure only relevant contact data is retrieved.

Step 4: Extract Sales Cloud Contacts - Extracting Contact Information

In this step, we process the contact data retrieved from SAP Sales Cloud V2, extract the relevant fields, and format them for further use in the integration. To achieve this, we use a Groovy script that structures the data according to SAP Emarsys' requirements. For simplicity, this example extracts only the first name, last name, and email address, but additional fields can be included as needed.

 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper;
import groovy.json.JsonOutput;

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def body = message.getBody(String)  
    def jsonSlurper = new JsonSlurper()
    def jsonResponse = jsonSlurper.parseText(body)  

    if (!jsonResponse || !jsonResponse.value || jsonResponse.value.isEmpty()) {
        throw new RuntimeException("No contact data found. API Response: " + body)
    }

    def contactsList = jsonResponse.value.collect { contact ->
        return [
            "3": contact.eMail ?: "",  
            "1": contact.givenName ?: "",  
            "2": contact.familyName ?: ""  
        ]
    }

    def emarsysPayload = [
        key_id: "3",
        contacts: contactsList
    ]

    message.setProperty("EmarsysBody", JsonOutput.toJson(emarsysPayload))  // Store entire JSON in a property

    return message;
}

 

We start by retrieving the API response as a string and parsing it into a JSON object. If no contacts are found, we throw an exception to prevent unnecessary API calls.

Next, we extract the key attributes needed for SAP Emarsys. In this example, we focus on:

  • Email Address (3)
  • First Name (1)
  • Last Name (2)

We iterate over the contact list and map each contact’s attributes into a structured format. If any of these fields are missing, we assign an empty string to ensure consistency in the data structure.

Once we have extracted the required fields, we structure the data into a JSON payload that SAP Emarsys can process. Since SAP Emarsys uses the email address as the unique identifier, we set the key_id to 3

Finally, we store the structured JSON in a message property (EmarsysBody), making it available for the next step in the iFlow.

Step 5: Configure SAP Emarsys Request

In this step, we prepare the data extracted from SAP Sales Cloud V2 for submission to the SAP Emarsys API. This involves setting up a Content Modifier and configuring the necessary OpenID Connect (OIDC, OAuth 2.0, JWT) based API authentication using the Integration Suite.

1. Add a Content Modifier

First, add a Content Modifier step in the iFlow after the Groovy script. The purpose of this step is to store the payload that will be sent to the SAP Emarsys API.

Before creating the new headers, delete the existing CamelHttpQuery header to avoid conflicts with the new request configuration. To do this, add a new row in the Message Header section and select Delete as the action. Enter CamelHttpQuery as the header name and leave the other fields empty.

Message Header

HTTP headers for Emarsys request and OAuth authentication

Next, we need to add the required headers for the OAuth 2.0 request and data exchange with the SAP Emarsys API:

  • Go to the Message Header tab.
  • Add first header:
    • Name: Content-Type
    • Source Type: Constant
    • Source Value: application/json
  • Add second header:
    • Name: Accept
    • Source Type: Constant
    • Source Value: application/json
Message Body

Next, we need to define the message body that will be sent to the SAP Emarsys API. This body contains the structured contact data extracted and formatted in the Groovy script:

  • Navigate to the Message Body tab.
  • Type: Expression 
  • Body: ${property.EmarsysBody} - we set this property in the groovy script.

2. Configure OAuth 2.0 Authentication

To connect to the SAP Emarsys API, you need to configure OAuth 2.0 authentication in the Integration Suite. This is done by setting up OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials in the Security Material.

Configuring Security Material for Emarsys Authentication

You can obtain the OAuth 2.0 credentials directly from SAP Emarsys or configure them using OpenID Connect with SAP Cloud Identity. For detailed steps on setting up credentials via OpenID Connect, refer to the official  SAP Emarsys documentation here.

Alternatively, you can create API credentials directly in SAP Emarsys. You can find the detailed steps here. Ensure that the Token Service URL is correctly set to the SAP Emarsys token endpoint.

When configuring OAuth 2.0 credentials in the Security Material of the Integration Suite, make sure to populate the SCOPE field (e.g.'api'). Missing scope values can lead to authentication errors. For more details on setting up OAuth 2.0 authentication, refer to this SAP Community blog.

Step 6: Create Contacts in SAP Emarsys

In this step, we create new contacts in SAP Emarsys using the extracted and formatted data from SAP Sales Cloud V2. To achieve this, we need to configure a Request Reply Step and an HTTP Receiver Adapter to send the contact data to SAP Emarsys.

1. Setting Up the Request Reply Step

  1. Add a Request Reply after the Content Modifier.
  2. Connect the Request Reply to an HTTP Receiver Adapter.

2. Configuring the HTTP Receiver Adapter

  • Address: The endpoint for creating contacts in SAP Emarsys: https://api.emarsys.net/api/v3/contact
  • Proxy Type: Internet
  • Method: POST
  • Authentication: OAuth2 Client Credentials
  • Credential Name: Your credential name from the previous step

At this point, the contact data extracted from SAP Sales Cloud V2 is ready to be created in SAP Emarsys. The HTTP Receiver Adapter sends the formatted data as a POST request to the SAP Emarsys API.

 

Validating the Integration

After setting up the iFlow and configuring all necessary steps, we can now test the integration to confirm that contact data is successfully transferred from SAP Sales Cloud V2 to SAP Emarsys.

Step 1: Create a Contact in SAP Sales Cloud V2

To validate the integration, we start by creating a new contact in SAP Sales Cloud V2. In this example, a contact named Emma Evans with the email address emma.evans@alpha.cx.demo.sap was created in SAP Sales Cloud V2.

Step 2: Verify That the Contact is Not Yet Available in SAP Emarsys

Next, we search for the newly created contact in SAP Emarsys using the email address. As shown in the screenshot, the search returns no results, confirming that the contact is not yet present in SAP Emarsys.

Step 3: Execute the Integration Flow

We then manually trigger the iFlow to push the newly created contact from SAP Sales Cloud V2 to SAP Emarsys. The iFlow sends a POST request to the SAP Emarsys API with the contact details extracted and formatted in the previous steps.

Step 4: Confirm That the Contact is Now Available in SAP Emarsys

Finally, we search for the contact in SAP Emarsys again using the email address. This time, the contact is successfully found, confirming that the data transfer was successful. The contact's first name, last name, and email address are correctly displayed in SAP Emarsys, indicating that the iFlow is working as expected.

 

Conclusion

In this post, we walked through the process of setting up an end-to-end integration between SAP Sales Cloud V2 and SAP Emarsys using the Cloud Integration component within the SAP Integration Suite. We started by configuring the connection to SAP Sales Cloud V2, filtering the data to retrieve only relevant contacts, and extracting the necessary fields using a Groovy script. We then prepared the data for SAP Emarsys, set up OpenID Connect (OIDC, OAuth 2.0, JWT) based API authentication, and sent the data to SAP Emarsys using an HTTP Receiver Adapter.

The successful execution of the integration was confirmed when the contact created in SAP Sales Cloud V2 appeared in SAP Emarsys after the process ran. This demonstrates how easily the SAP Integration Suite can be used to connect SAP Sales Cloud V2 and SAP Emarsys - laying the foundation for more complex processes in the future. 

By following these steps, you can build a similar integration in your environment, adapting the filter conditions and field mapping to meet your specific business needs.

entry.title: Re: DOX Premium Prompting: Best Practices & Hints
entry.content:
Hi Emanuele, 

have you tried setting the data type to String? 

You can also reach out to me directly via mail (christoph.batke@sap.com) if you want to discuss in further detail. 

Best, 

Christoph

entry.title: Fixed Asset History Sheet
entry.content:
Hi All

Is it possible to edit the System Fixed Asset History Sheet to filter by Capitalization Acquisition's Posting Date instead of Asset Value Date?  

Our client has postings in Acquisition with Posting Date later than Asset Value Date.

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: Re: Savely secure Credentials in ABAP
entry.content:
Hi,
as mentioned by abo and my previos link -> better use Secure Store and Forward Mechanism (SSF) (SAP Library - Secure Programming)

entry.title: Re: Repricing at VF04
entry.content: Hi Ravi, that sound wired; you should check the pricing_copy and pricing BadIs

entry.title: Unable to Clear Multiple Documents at Once in FB05
entry.content:
I’m facing an issue when clearing multiple documents at once using FB05.

When I select and clear each document pair individually, the clearing process works correctly. However, when I select multiple documents together, the system does not clear the entire amount. Instead, only a portion of the selected documents get cleared, leaving an unexpected balance.

I checked OB74 (Automatic Clearing Rules) to ensure relevant fields (e.g., ZUONR, GSBER, VBOUND, VERTN, VBEWA, UMSKZ) are consistent across documents. And no locked documents.

If there are any system restrictions preventing multiple documents from being cleared at once in FB05?

Pls help me.

entry.title: Re: How to restrict Visibility(edit or view) of HRIS elem...
entry.content: Thanks for the advice and attachment😊

entry.title: APM In-house banking Interest calculation
entry.content:
Hello,

I have implemented APM  in the S4Hana 2023 version for intercompany payments, the whole process works, but I cannot calculate the interest on the balance.
I have read the suggested notes FQA, the help.sap, the configuration manual, but I cannot solve it.

Settings customizing

  1. Define G/L Accounts for In-House Banking
  2. Define Activity Types for Account Management
  3. Maintain Activity Type Criteria for Account Management

Mater data

1. conditions created

2. Internal bank account update 

3. End of day done

4. Bank statement sent 

5. GL posting done

Regards,

Paula

entry.title: Re: Italy eInvoice submission to SDI
entry.content: Do you know if there is a specific standard field into Vendor and Customer Master data in which store the SDI account identification number?

entry.title: Re: is there a way to maintain or upload custom css files...
entry.content:
Hi Mahesh,

I understood your question imply that the integrated CSS options are not enough for you. If this understanding is correct: There is no easy solution out of the box.

However, you can freely use JavaScript in a custom widget (or in a widget add-on) and thus also freely change or add CSS styling. So that´s quite a one time effort, but then you´re free to do whatever you want.

Best regards,
Denis

PS: Please set as accepted answer, if this answers your question, thanks.

entry.title: Re: Import multiple bpmn to SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Hi,
You can import multiple BPMNs at once using our API and a script/middleware:

  • API Guide
  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  • Postman Collections

Best regards
Manuel

entry.title: Re: Setup Synthetic user Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM with On-Prem Runner
entry.content: @Richard_Büffing Thanks for a great blog. This blog is really helpful for us. I have one doubt, can't we use other Webdriver like wdi5 or qmate for CALM Synthetic User Monitoring? In the documentation, it mentioned only selenium ide, just wondering whether it would be possible.

entry.title: Schedule jobs for consolidation tasks with dynamic period
entry.content:
Is is possible to use the schedule for consolidations tasks app to release data each month for the current month? We are looking to create a recurring job that release data from universal journal on the 5th day of each month for the previous month. However, when setting the parameters we still have to set a month, see yellow marker.

What I would be looking for is a an option to say that the data should be release for the previous month. So when the job is executed in February, it should release data for Jan of the current year. Is that possible?

And what does the option highlighted in blue do?

Thanks,
Arnold

Siddu

entry.title: Re: Joule speaks ABAP!

entry.title: Re: Calculating Energy Consumption in the Product Carbon Footprint
entry.content:
Hello,

While fiddling around with Allocations and Energy Flow Models, I realized I should probably just go with CCFs to get the Carbon Footprint per Product. Seems my approach was wrong.

Thanks again for your help!

entry.title: Re: Sales rebate agreement for a single customer based on...
entry.content:
Hello,

I'd like to ask further, we want to have a calculation type/condition for a fixed amount, and we want that this fixed amount is somehow spread/allocated proportionally to the quantities of each material (SKU) in an invoice per customer. E.g. if the fixed amount is 100 Eur for the single/independent customer and there are 5 invoices for the other/different customers, and each invoice has for simplicity one material/SKU, so the 100 Eur is allocated proportionally to these 5 SKUs, so 20 EUR per SKU per customer. Can the CCM do that?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Calculating Energy Consumption in the Product Carbon Footprint
entry.content:
Hello,

yes, that is the ultimate intended goal. I think I will have to give up on doing it via PCFs and go with the CCFs as I realized that is probably the best approach for this during my conversation with @die_ste.

Thanks for sharing your approach! This is what I am going to do moving forward.

entry.title: Re: Printing a single page from multiple pages using Crystal Reports Viewer
entry.content: Hi, DonWilliams. Thank you for your reply. We will send an image of the Windows Print dialog opened from our app. It opens when we click the Print button of the CR Viewer embedded in a VB.NET Form. Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere - Populating Dynamic Default Values in Analytical Model Variables
entry.content:
As @Nitin_Patni wrote, intervals are not yet possible in derived values.

A way to work around this:
In your temporal view, you could have two colums, one for your current date and one for the date two years ago.

In your AM you then have to use two filter variables on the billing date, one for start and one for end.

entry.title: Re: Calculating Energy Consumption in the Product Carbon Footprint
entry.content: Hi, thank you for the interesting roadmap! I think I understand what you mean, although my understanding is not quite deep enough to properly respond, I will keep this in mind. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio process manager - attribute mass changes
entry.content: Hello, I see that this Help Documentation is not accessible currently. Could you please check if you have any other related Documentation

entry.title: Posting change to transfer SO stock is incorrectly asking for a reason for movement
entry.content:
When completing a posting change in EWM to move batch of 913108 from SO 5044582 to 5044581, the system would not proceed without a reason for movement being entered. 
Once entered, the Posting Change processed and updated in both EWM and IM - movement type 413.
A reason for movement was not required before - and should not be needed for this movement type

Attachment shows the error and the MB51 completed transactions after the reason for movement was added

which link is not accessible? The first one requires login and a S-User, the second and third should be accessible, I just tested it. 
Edit: actually all 3 links are accessible without login. The single link shared above is outdated and should not be further used, you might require an S-User to access it.

Best regards
Manuel

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud Edition: Email status 'sent' in app: Mon...
entry.content:
I hit the same problem when sending test mail via "Monitor Email Transmission " app.

I read 1LQ setup guide and performed settings of below only. (i think other are not necessary for a simple email function test)

Defining an Email Sender Domain. 
Defining a Default Email Sender Address
Defining Email Receiver Addresses

for DKIM, it is required for SAP S/4 HANA Private Cloud Edition only.

Still checking, if no clue then may need to do below according to 3496200....

  • If the system is on SAP Public Cloud and the mail server is administrated by SAP, create case under component XX-S4C-OPR-INC .

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud Edition: Email status 'sent' in app: Mon...
entry.content: I hit the same problem when sending test mail via "Monitor Email Transmission" app.

entry.title: Carrier selection based on gross weight ?
entry.content:
Dear SAP TM experts,

I have a requirement from my customer and I am not sure it could be managed through standard features. Hereunder a few lines for this business requirement:

The customer wants to perform the carrier selection taking into account the gross weight of the truck. This weight limit can be different in function of the route of the trucks.

For example

From point A to point B, available carriers are

Carrier 1 between 10 and 25 tons

Carrier 2 between 10 and 25 tons

Carrier 3 for all the trucks under 10 tons.

 

From point A to point C, available carriers are

Carrier 1 between 12 and 25 tons

Carrier 2 between 12 and 25 tons

Carrier 4 for trucks under 12 tons.

 

In order to assign the carrier to the geographical attributes, the transportation lane seems to fit perfectly the requirement. On the other hand, it does not seem to handle such weight limitations.

I investigated also allocations but this object seems to be apply a limite on a period of time, not on each Freight Order.

A last possibility would maybe to deal with incompatibilities but i'm affraid to complexify a lot the solution for the users.

Did you already face this requirement ? Or maybe can you please suggest how you would handle it? 

Thank you for your help.

Best regards,

AdrienM

entry.title: Re: Removing comp planner name as suffix from Worksheet
entry.content: @Ritanshi, these functions are not applicable to Compensation and Variable Pay (I think they are applicable to Performance Management)

entry.title: Re: Inline translations do not work properly

I've searched on the SAP website and Opentext website but see none.  Do you know where there maybe a demo?  

Thank you for any help or suggestion!

Cheryl

entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I would like to get the current date time of specific time zone (eg. AU time zone) in the BPA.

I used new Date() and it gets as GMT +0 time zone.

How can I convert to AU time zone?

 

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Re: Method 'CREATE_STREAM' not implemented in data provider class( in RAP Application)
entry.content:
I have tried adding the file upload feature to my CDS i'm using Odata V2 like you and i can't even choose the file that i can upload it's like my field where i can upload the file is disabled when it is not.

I have checked everything and tried everything i even went so far by disabling the virus scan but always the same issue.
I started to wonder if it is even possible to implement the file upload feature for an Odata V2.

entry.title: INTRASTAT - goods from different country than the vendor how to exclude from report
entry.content: Hi how can we exclude from intrastat report cases in which the vendor lets say from Germany provides  goods lets say from Great Britain so it should not be in Intrastat report (because GB is outside EU) but it is because as per the vendor it is from Germany so we have to manually exclude it. Or other case how can we exclude in third party because lets say we buy goods from a country but physically it never reaches our country because we sell it further to our mother company in a different country but for different reasons we probably have to do GR and GI.

entry.title: Sap Cap Java does not process CDS files same way as nodejs
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Based on SAP CAP documentation in this link: 

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/using-services#mashing-up-with-remote-services

It seems to handle expands in Java we must take care of
request in our handler like in example:
https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-cap-risk-management/blob/ext-service-s4hc-suppliers-ui-java/srv/src/main/java/com/sap/cap/riskmanagement/handler/RiskServiceHandler.java

But when we use this use case in nodejs, there is no
need to handle expands. It is handled by CAP libraries.
We just need to bind to Suppliers read event and send
the incoming CQN query to remote service and it is
done.


But in this way we need to also take care of nested
relations and there will be many boilerplate code. Is
there a library to handle CQN queries like in nodejs ?

entry.title: Re: Investment Support Measures for Assets SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello!

In new release 2502, there is functionality to manage grants (investmet support measures) in assets??

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio process manager - attribute mass changes
entry.content: This link: https://help.sap.com/doc/sap-signavio-process-manager-api-documentation/SHIP/en-US/rest_api/index.html is outdated. You need to login via S-User to see it. However the same content is available here with new API endpoints: https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-manager/sap-signavio-process-manager-api/sap-signavio-process-manager-api-guide

entry.title: Re: Ariba Network + VIM in China via Baiwang

entry.title: Native driver not recognised
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to update my ADS system. Right when starting SUM I get the following error message:

 

I checked, and the driver is installed:

I also checked that I unpacked and started SUM with the SIDADM user as this caused some problems in the past.

Do you know what I need to check or edit to get SUM to use the driver?

I don't want to change the whole system to this driver, as ADS is using the SAP Standard driver.

entry.title: Re: Query-Based Approval Not Triggering Re-Approval in Sa...
entry.content:
Hi @sreesajs ,

In the version we are using here (2502), this is working fine. See our setup below:

The query we are using is very simple: 

SELECT DISTINCT 'TRUE' 
FROM OPRQ 
WHERE $[$OPRQ.DocTotal] > 10000

And if any element of the approved draft is changed by the originator, the approval flow is triggered again until the approved draft is finally posted without any changes.

You may try something similar in your test environment to isolate the root cause, whether it is the setup, query or even SAP version.

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: ControllerExtension.use(EditFlow) with typescript does not work
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm using typescript in my Fiori app and like to use EditFlow extension . I have a problem in initiating the EditFlow extension . 

 

 

import ControllerExtension from "sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension";
import EditFlow from "sap/fe/core/controllerextensions/EditFlow";
import Controller from "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller";

/**
 * @namespace paltr.core.configsystemkind
 */
export default class SetSystemKindsTitlesCustomAction extends Controller {
  private editFlow: EditFlow;
  public onInit(): void {

        this.editFlow = ControllerExtension.use(EditFlow);
    }
   ....
}

 

 

I'm not using the Babel plugin, I'm using the ui5-tooling.transpile version 3.7.2 instead as described in the Fiori documentation section "Assigning controller extension" Using Controller Extension - Documentation - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK

The mention "npm update" was executed.

My issue is ControllerExtension.use(EditFlow) throws the error "

Argument of type 'typeof EditFlow' is not assignable to parameter of type 'new () => EditFlow'.
Cannot assign a 'protected' constructor type to a 'public' constructor type.".
 
How do I call the extension correctly in typescript?

entry.title: Re: Posting Net Pay to Two Different GL Accounts
entry.content: Create two symbolic accounts and map the components you need ,, and assign those symbolic to two GL .

entry.title: Re: Add Relations in Operation using 'BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN'
entry.content:
Try calling BAPI_ALM_ORDER_GET_DETAIL to get some examples of data formats.

Also

  • Look at function modules such as MAP2E_RELATION_TO_EXTERN from the IBAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAPPING function group.
  • Check in table AUFK for AUFNR = 000004000001 or 000004001664 the values of field  AUTYP (20 = Network, 30 = PM order)

entry.title: New Cloud Outage Notification Subscription User Experience in SAP for Me!
entry.content:
The tool is designed to enhance the user experience for Cloud Outage Notification Subscription in SAP for Me. Here are the key capabilities:

  1. Subscription Management: Users, including regular s-users, Cloud Administrators, and Super Administrators, can create, display, change, or delete their own subscriptions for authorized Cloud Services and Systems.
  2. Creating New Subscriptions: Users can easily create new subscriptions by selecting "Add New Subscription" and choosing the type of subscription (Cloud Service or System). Multiple services can be selected, and the tool provides options to configure notification types such as Disruption/Degradation, Maintenance, Major Upgrade, and Customer Communication.
  3. Administrator Capabilities: Cloud Administrators and Super Administrators can manage subscriptions for other users, including non-s-users who can receive notifications but cannot access SAP for Me to manage their subscriptions.
  4. User-Friendly Interface: The tool offers a user-friendly interface with features like free text search, filters, and a clear display of selected items and conditions. It also provides insights through a question mark icon.
  5. Upcoming Features: The tool will soon include a feature for subscription on bundles, enhancing its functionality further.

 

Path in SAP for Me: Systems & Provisioning dashboard / Get Notified tab 

 

My Subscriptions (default screen)

As a regular s-user, Cloud Administrator, or Super Administrator, you can create, display, change or delete your own subscriptions for authorized Cloud Services and Systems.

 

To create a new subscription, select Add New Subscription in the top right corner.

 

In the popup, choose a Cloud Service or System subscription type.

If you are a Super Administrator with access to multiple entities of your Customer hierarchy, select one.

After selecting Cloud Service in the first filter, choose one or more Cloud Services in the subsequent popup.

To achieve this, you may utilize the Search text field or apply the available filters. Please be advised that we have updated the Product Group selection to Solution Area, to align the experience with other areas of SAP for Me. Once your selection is complete, please click OK.

 

 

Multiple services can be selected. The Selected Items and Conditions area will reflect your selection, as well as the number of Cloud Services chosen.

 

Click OK to confirm your choice and go back to the Add New Subscription screen.

Choose your Notifications Type Configuration: Disruption/Degradation, Maintenance, Major Upgrade, Customer Communication. For the initial three types, you will have the option to unsubscribe from certain alerts.

 

 

Click the question mark for insights.

 

After saving, your new notification will appear at the top of the list. Selecting multiple Cloud Services will create multiple subscriptions.

Clicking on the row will allow you to display and edit the Notification Type Configuration.

 

Creating a System level notification directs you to a screen to select specific systems, with two dedicated BTP filters available.

 

Depending on your system landscaped, the System Role may include:

 

Manage Subscriptions

As a Cloud Administrator or Super Administrator, you can create, display, change or delete subscriptions for other users. These users can be s-users or non-s-users. The non-s-users will be able to receive notifications, but not to access SAP for Me and manage their subscriptions.

In the Add Subscription screen, the Administrator selects one or more contacts along with the other fields.

 

 

The free text search will find the required contact(s) for the subscription.

 

In the main screen two perspectives are provided: By Subscriptions and By Subscribers.

 

By Subscriptions

Click on a Subscription to view the list of Subscribers.

 

By Subscribers

The User Type filter lets you choose between S-Users and Non-S-Users. While Non-S-Users can't access SAP for Me, an Administrator user can create subscriptions for them.

 

Click a Subscriber to view their subscriptions.

 

You have just learned how to use the new Cloud Outage Notification Subscription tool in SAP for Me!. Feedback is highly appreciated. Please use the comment section for that.

If you are interested in upcoming features, please follow my profile and the SAP for Me tag.

entry.title: Deployment is in Pending Status since 3 days in SAP AI launchpad - ML Deployment
entry.content:
Greetings Community Members!

Kindly help me with understanding on Deployment troubleshooting.  I have deployed a vision model using Postman, it was working well then I made some changes in re-deployed again. It has been in pending status since last 3 days.  Is there any ways to know Logs, debug, or troubleshoot deployment process ? in AI launchpad - ML( Deployment Section) Logs are very basic not providing more information,  is there any way we can see more detail logs for the deployment ?

Thank you
Best Regards
Naim 

 SAP AI Core

 # AI Launchpad 

#SAP ML Deployments

entry.title: Mass Data Changes for Master Data (General Ledger Accounts, etc.) Using SAP Fiori Apps
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

In the past, I used LSMW on R3 to change master data of G/L accounts or other FI objects en masse. As far as I know, there is the SAP Migration Cockpit as a successor to LSMW, but it can only be used to migrate new master data records for G/L accounts, for example.
I am looking for a Fiori app to change masses of existing master data of, for example, G/L accounts or other FI objects.
Can anyone help?
Thanks in advance.

entry.title: how can i restrict the equipment that can be seen by users based on cost centers ?
entry.content:
Hello,

My client is using SAP public cloud, we are having a very weird problem on maintenance transactions : 

- Find technical objects

- Change technical object

we restricted the maintenance users using the restriction object "Cost center" in "General",

they can only see the equipment that have 4 specific cost centers, now here is the problem, some equipment don't have a cost center they have a WBS element instead, what we noticed is that those equipment with the empty field "cost center" can't be seen by the users on the transaction "Find technical object" but they can see it in "change technical object".

 

So basically, if the equipment has no cost center, they will not be able to access it only from the transaction change technical object.

anyone know why this is happening ?

 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: About SAP B1 10.00.120 WEB CLIENT OPEN IS BLANK PAGE
entry.content: When I open web clinet, I am prompted that sapui is out of date, as shown in the picture. What should I do to access web clinet normally?

entry.title: About sap b1 sdk use
entry.content:
private void OnCustomInitialize()
{
oForm = (SAPbouiCOM.Form)sbo_app.Forms.ActiveForm;


if (oForm.DataSources.DataTables.Count == 0)
{
dt = oForm.DataSources.DataTables.Add("dt");
}
else
{
try
{
dt = oForm.DataSources.DataTables.Item("dt");
}
catch
{
dt = oForm.DataSources.DataTables.Add("dt");
}
}
string query = "SELECT u_t001 FROM [@itemproperties2]";
dt.ExecuteQuery(query);


Matrix0.Clear();


SAPbouiCOM.Column column = Matrix0.Columns.Item("cl_a");
column.DataBind.Bind("dt", "u_t001");


Matrix0.LoadFromDataSource();


if (Matrix0.RowCount > 0)
{

 


Matrix0.SelectRow(1, true, false);
}
}

I want to ask how to set martix to load data when it is opened? I set up the data source and bind data to the corresponding column, why is it not automatically loaded when it is opened, or why is it not displayed?

entry.title: Re: WEBI ERORR while opening dashboard
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I just found your message while I was getting the same error (after platform update) : this error is occuring after a document is promoted.

I know this is a brand new document in your case, but I share this Note SAP 3496871 in case of someone else reading us.

entry.title: Availability check for KMAT
entry.content:
Hi,

 

I've got an issue with availability check for KMAT. We've got in our company collective order implemented. Collective order consists of configurable materials. 

Main material is configurable and suborders are also based on configurable materials. 

We're facing an issue with creating delivery notes. Sales order consists of many items.

Scenario: Item 10 and 50 are already confirmed, delivered on stock. Sales people create partial delivery note. They do not check what was produced, they just create a delivery note. SAP allows them to create delivery note with items that were not produced. They can create delivery note i.e for items 20,30,50. 

How can I set the settings for availability check to prevent this problem? I would like that system takes into account only items which production orders were confirmed. 

Item category in sales order - STANDARD MTO. Schedule line category: CP.

Availability check in material master for delivery notes takes into account safety stock and include deliveries.

Does anybody encounter the same problem and has a solution? 

 

Regards,

MM

entry.title: Re: Name of iOS app is not showing correctly on iOS and MacOS
entry.content:
Dear Pekka,

Glad to see the progress!

The issue is that the app is already on the appstore since two years and that is the name of the app so I cannot change it. Hope you can fix the issue asap, thank you!

Interesting to understand why this problem happened while before there was no problem.

Best regards,

Chantal

entry.title: How to Add a Filter in SAP Cloud RESTful Application Programming Without Affecting Data Retrieval?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am working on a Fiori Elements application and want to add a new filter parameter (e.g., CustomDateFilter as shown in the screenshot). However, I don’t want this parameter to impact the actual data retrieval when clicking the Go button. Instead, I need it for additional processing or logic within the application.

How can I implement this in SAP Fiori Elements or RAP without affecting the dataset returned from the backend? Are there any best practices for handling such scenarios? (By the way i am using Restful Application Programming).

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Adopting SuccessFactors Talent intelligence hub: Where to Begin?
entry.content:
Dear @RinkyKarthik, 

Thank you for the useful blog post. I am also interested in the ALP for TIH, so looking forward to your update(s) on this, when you have it ready for us 🙂  Warm regards, Emma

entry.title: Re: SAP PS Activity Error Encountered During Period Closing
entry.content: After confirming the approved network plan activity, we check the COFC screen and see that this confirmation is incorrect. While performing the CO material ledger period closing, the closing cannot be completed due to this network plan.

entry.title: Re: SAP PS Activity Error Encountered During Period Closing
SOST emails sent from which program
entry.content: Is there a way or a table which I can find the program that is generating the emails being sent out in SOST?
